authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.41 — Landlocked Salmon
sources.quotedText: (a) Open season: All year. (b) Daily bag limit: Five. (c) Possession limit: Ten. (d) Size limit: None. (e) Exceptions: (1) Bucks Lake (Plumas Co.), New Bullards Bar Reservoir (Yuba Co.), Pardee Lake (Amador Co.), and Trinity Lake (Trinity Co.), which shall be subject to the following limits: (A) Daily bag limit: Ten. (B) Possession limit: Twenty.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Don Pedro Reservoir not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Don Pedro Reservoir not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R4 Tuolumne Don Pedro Reservoir Brown Trout Sub-catchable Put and Grow January-April & October-December; R4 Tuolumne Don Pedro Reservoir Chinook Salmon Fingerling Put and Grow April-June; R4 Tuolumne Don Pedro Reservoir Kokanee Salmon Fingerling Put and Grow April-June; R4 Tuolumne Don Pedro Reservoir Rainbow Trout Catchable Put and Take January-April & October-December

parameters.note: Don Pedro Reservoir (Lake Don Pedro) is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1). Kokanee and Chinook (landlocked salmon) are likewise open all year under §5.41(a).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, counted as the total number of trout in combination. Kokanee and Chinook salmon are regulated separately under §5.41 and do not count against the trout limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit (all trout species in combination)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: Kokanee and Chinook (landlocked salmon) are regulated under §5.41, separately from trout under §5.85, and are not counted against the trout bag/possession limit. Don Pedro Reservoir is not among the §5.41(e) exception waters (Bucks Lake, New Bullards Bar Reservoir, Pardee Lake, Trinity Lake), so the general five/ten landlocked-salmon limit applies.

regulations.humanSummary: Kokanee and Chinook (landlocked salmon): 5 daily bag limit, 10 possession limit — a separate limit from trout
regulations.verbatimText: (a) Open season: All year. (b) Daily bag limit: Five. (c) Possession limit: Ten. (d) Size limit: None.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Don Pedro Reservoir (Region 4, Tuolumne Co.) for sub-catchable-size Brown Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of January-April & October-December (a split range that does not fit a single start-end month window). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Don Pedro Reservoir (Region 4, Tuolumne Co.) for fingerling-size Chinook Salmon under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of April-June. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Don Pedro Reservoir (Region 4, Tuolumne Co.) for fingerling-size Kokanee Salmon under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of April-June. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Don Pedro Reservoir (Region 4, Tuolumne Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of January-April & October-December (a split range that does not fit a single start-end month window). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
